title: Residential, day and domiciliary care managers and proprietors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate academic or nonacademic activities of preschools or childcare centers and programs, including before- and after-school care. Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, public health agencies, or similar organizations. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Plan and administer budgets for programs, equipment, and support services.
Recruit, interview, and hire or sign up volunteers and staff.
Speak to community groups to explain and interpret agency purposes, programs, and policies.
Research and analyze member or community needs to determine program directions and goals.
Participate in formulating organizational policies regarding participant eligibility, program requirements, and benefits.
Evaluate the work of staff and volunteers to ensure programs are of appropriate quality and resources are used effectively.
Prepare and maintain records and reports such as budgets and personnel records.
Establish and maintain relationships with other agencies to meet community needs and ensure service efficiency.
Implement and evaluate training programs for staff, volunteers, or the community.
Provide direct service and support to clients, handling referrals and resolving complaints.
Review and evaluate programs to determine their efficiency and compliance with regulations, recommending modifications as needed.
Conduct recruitment, hiring, and training of personnel for programs and services.
Plan and administer programs and services in a medical facility including personnel administration and staff coordination.
Monitor and evaluate resources to ensure effective use and assess the need for additional staff or services.
Prepare activity reports to inform management on the status of programs and service initiatives.
Review financial statements and other performance data to measure productivity and identify areas for improvement.
Direct administrative activities related to providing services.
Develop and implement organizational policies for the facility or medical unit.
